body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
irrlicht_observes: partial

`applicable` resolves to `yes` on the matrix algebra (partial/partial), but the cell degrades to recording-not-applicable: there is no deterministic, non-faked way to drive a real quota-exhaustion episode in a bounded live recording, and even if a provider 429 occurred, pi masks it (no distinct observable state).

## What the scenario wants

From scenario-meanings: the agent hits a hard token/cost cap mid-turn, emits a turn-end signal, the session resolves `working â†’ ready`, and subsequent prompts stay `ready` until the budget resets. The canonical primitive is *budget enforcement* â€” the CLI itself enforces a token/time budget and emits a turn-end signal when the cap is hit.

## agent_supports: partial

pi tracks per-turn usage/cost (parser.go:107-113 reads `message.usage`; extractPiContribution prefers the provider-reported `cost`), and the footer surfaces a running cost figure. But pi enforces NO self-imposed hard cap: `pi --help` (0.70.0) exposes no `--max-tokens`, `--budget`, `--cost-limit`, or any session-aborting cap flag (the only `Limit` mentions are model-cycling globs). pi cannot self-abort a turn at a token budget. The only way a quota episode arises is an upstream provider 429 â€” and pi's underlying `@mariozechner/pi-ai` SDK auto-retries transient HTTP failures transparently inside its request layer, so a 429 does not surface as a distinct turn-end. Hence `partial`: pi tracks cost/usage (the metric half of the primitive) but does not enforce a budget (the enforcement half), and provider-side quota is auto-retried away.

## irrlicht_observes: partial

The pi adapter (FilesUnderRoot ~/.pi/agent/sessions) tails an append-only JSONL transcript. The parser (parser.go) recognizes exactly: `session`, `model_change`, `compaction` (â†’ assistant/working), `message`/assistant with `stopReason=="stop"` (â†’ turn_done/ready) vs non-stop (â†’ assistant/working), `user`, `toolResult` (with `isError`). There is NO quota-exhausted, rate-limit, or provider-error event kind. A quota episode would have to manifest as either (a) an ordinary `stopReason==stop` turn-end (indistinguishable from a normal turn completing) or (b) the process exiting (`process_exited`). irrlicht would observe the resulting `working â†’ ready` arc, but nothing in the transcript marks it as quota-driven â€” so the observable is identical to any normal turn end. `partial`: the lifecycle arc is observable; the *quota* nature of it is not.

## Why this cell is recording-not-applicable (degrade to applicable:false)

There is no genuine, deterministic, non-faked trigger:

1. **No self-cap flag.** Verified against `pi --help` 0.70.0 and the dist bundle â€” pi has no `--max-tokens`/budget/cost-limit flag that self-aborts a session. So we cannot make pi end a turn at a token budget on demand.
2. **Real provider 429 is not inducible on demand**, and the project rule forbids faking/injecting transcript lines.
3. **The Anthropic-429 mock path doesn't transfer.** claudecode records this cell via tools/agent-onboarding/recording/mock-anthropic-429/main.go, but that mock launches `claude --bare` (reading ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL); there is no pi equivalent. Even if pi were pointed at an Anthropic-compatible 429 mock, pi-ai auto-retries the 429 internally and pi writes nothing distinct to the JSONL for a quota event â€” so the curated fixture would contain only ordinary working/ready arcs with no quota signal to assert against.

This mirrors the codex sibling verdict (codex/token-quota-exhausted is also applicable:false: real quota cannot be burned on demand, no codex 429 mock, no driver injection primitive). For pi the gap is even cleaner: there is no quota-exhausted observable in the transcript AT ALL, so the cell is unrecordable as specified regardless of trigger.

The committed expected.jsonl documents the TARGET arc (a working span that ends in ready without sticking, no waiting) should pi ever ship a self-enforced budget cap OR surface a distinct quota signal in its transcript.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: even if a real 429 occurred, pi's transcript carries no quota/rate-limit/provider-error line â€” the only observable is an ordinary workingâ†’ready arc, indistinguishable from a normal turn end.
pi-ai auto-retries provider 429s transparently inside the SDK request layer, so a quota response does not surface as a distinct turn-end signal.
pi 0.70.0 has no self-imposed token/cost budget cap flag (no --max-tokens / --budget / --cost-limit); pi cannot self-abort a turn at a budget on demand.

sources.note: Parser recognizes session/model_change/compaction/message(stopâ†’turn_done, non-stopâ†’assistant)/user/toolResult(isError) â€” no quota/rate-limit/provider-error event kind.
Source = FilesUnderRoot ~/.pi/agent/sessions (append-only JSONL); the daemon only sees what pi writes to that file.

codex/token-quota-exhausted is applicable:false (real quota not burnable on demand, no codex 429 mock, no driver injection); claudecode uses the Anthropic-only mock-anthropic-429 launching claude --bare â€” neither path transfers to pi.
Mock is Anthropic-only and launches claude --bare; there is no pi equivalent, and pi-ai would auto-retry the 429 with no distinct transcript line.
No --max-tokens/--budget/--cost-limit/quota-cap flag; only model-cycling 'Limit' globs. Confirms pi has no self-enforced budget cap.
